Tests the behavior of Math.max with NaN as an argument.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the `Math.max` function when one of its arguments is `NaN`. The `Math.max` function is expected to return `NaN` when any of its arguments is `NaN`, and the `assert` function checks if this condition is true. This script provides narrow functionality by focusing on a specific test case for the `Math.max` function. It is useful for validating that the JavaScript environment correctly implements the expected behavior of `Math.max` with `NaN` inputs.
